endpoints.description: Health check endpoint for readiness probes and frontend connectivity test
Upload a PDF file via multipart/form-data, extract text with pdf-parse, store in memory, return session metadata

errors.body: { "error": "This file isn't a PDF. Please upload a PDF document." }
{ "error": "This file is over 20 MB. Try a smaller PDF." }
{ "error": "Could not read this PDF. The file may be corrupted or password-protected." }

endpoints.description: Send a message about an uploaded document and receive a streaming AI response via SSE. Uses Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile model.

errors.body: { "error": "Message is too long. Please keep it under 10,000 characters." }
{ "error": "Session not found. Please upload your PDF again." }
{ "error": "This PDF is too large for the AI to process. Try a shorter document." }
{ "error": "Too many requests. Wait a moment and try again." }

features: Express app with CORS (configurable via CORS_ORIGIN env, defaults to http://localhost:3000)
JSON body parser
Multer config: memory storage, single file, 20MB limit
GROQ_API_KEY validated on startup (console.warn if missing)
Global error handler middleware
Listens on PORT env var, defaults to 3001
Validates file presence and mimetype (application/pdf)
Wraps pdf-parse in try/catch for corrupted/password-protected PDFs (422)
Generates sessionId via crypto.randomUUID()
Stores { text, filename, pageCount } in session store
Returns { sessionId, filename, pageCount, charCount }
Validates sessionId existence, message presence, message length (max 10,000 chars)
Truncates PDF text to ~8000 tokens (estimated at 4 chars/token) with truncation notice
System prompt includes full (or truncated) PDF text with instruction to reference document
Handles empty PDF text case (suggests user try PDF with selectable text)
History role whitelist: only 'user' and 'assistant' roles accepted (prevents system prompt injection)
Groq API stream created BEFORE setting SSE headers (errors return proper JSON, not broken SSE)
Groq free-tier 413 rate_limit_exceeded distinguished from true context-length 413
SSE streaming: data: JSON per chunk, final event with done:true
Stream errors caught and sent as SSE event with error message
In-memory Map with TTL-based eviction (1 hour session lifetime)
Periodic cleanup every 10 minutes via setInterval
get() checks TTL on access and auto-deletes expired sessions
Exports set, get, has functions
Catches multer LIMIT_FILE_SIZE (413) and LIMIT_UNEXPECTED_FILE (400)
Generic 500 for unhandled errors — logs message, no stack trace to client
Node 20 Alpine base
npm ci --only=production
Copies src/ directory
Exposes port 3001
CMD: node src/server.js
Backend service: port 3001, GROQ_API_KEY from .env, CORS_ORIGIN set to http://localhost:3000
Frontend service: port 3000, depends_on backend, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL build arg
Both services: restart unless-stopped
